name: general-purpose
purpose: SoNash project-aware general-purpose agent for research, code search, and multi-step tasks with full SoNash project context including stack versions, security boundaries, and architecture patterns.
sanitize_fields: description (SoNash-specific)
body (entire body is SoNash-specific constraints: protected collections, httpsCallable, node:test, lib/firestore-service.ts, types/schemas.ts — all SoNash-only)
notes: Has 'skills: [sonash-context]' frontmatter field. model: inherit (inherits from spawner/caller, same as debugger). maxTurns: 30. No tools or disallowedTools fields specified in frontmatter — relies on default tool grant (full toolset). Body is entirely SoNash-specific; nothing in the body is portable. This is a 'SoNash-context injection' agent — its value is encoding project constraints, not a reusable pattern. The body references 65 skills / 57 agents / 14 pre-commit checks counts which will drift. Anti-patterns section (no docs, no refactor beyond scope, no Jest mocks) is generically useful but SoNash-framed.

name: git-flow-manager
purpose: Git Flow workflow manager for automating branch creation, merging, validation, release management, and pull request generation following Git Flow conventions.
notes: Has 'skills: [sonash-context]' frontmatter field. Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, Edit, Write. No disallowedTools, color, or maxTurns. model: sonnet. Body is entirely project-agnostic Git Flow methodology — branch hierarchy, naming conventions, merge strategy, PR generation, CHANGELOG, semver. Single SoNash reference only in example ('feature/notifications' work) — not in the methodology. The workflow examples (feature/release/hotfix commands) are copy-paste portable. One of the most portable agents in the entire roster. Already useful as JASON-OS general infrastructure. Uses gh CLI for PR creation — external dep. Status reporting format with ASCII art is portable. Commit message format includes '🤖 Generated with Claude Code' — standard co-authorship.

The SoNash MCP Infrastructure section names SoNash-specific servers (sonarcloud-server.js). Windows cmd /c npx wrapping pattern is platform-specific but relevant to JASON-OS Windows install. The general MCP lifecycle (add server → add permissions → health check → restart) is portable methodology.

name: media-analyst
purpose: Parallel analysis agent dispatched by /media-analysis skill. Receives transcript text, media metadata, and home context. Produces Creator View (6 sections) and Engineer View (6 dimension scores with 4-band system).
sanitize_fields: body (references CONVENTIONS.md Section 3 and Section 4 — SoNash-specific conventions doc)
body (home context inputs: SESSION_CONTEXT.md, ROADMAP.md — SoNash-specific paths)
notes: Has 'skills: [sonash-context]' frontmatter field. disallowedTools: Agent. model: sonnet. maxTurns: 15. Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob. Structural twin to document-analyst (D2a) — same 6-section Creator View + 6-dimension Engineer View pattern, same CONVENTIONS.md dependency, same 4-band scoring system (Critical/Needs Work/Healthy/Excellent). Both reference same dependency on CONVENTIONS.md Sections 3+4. The Creator View 6 sections (What This Content Understands, Relevant To Your Work, Where Approach Differs, The Challenge, Knowledge Candidates, What's Worth Avoiding) are portable analysis patterns. The 4-band scoring dimensions differ from document-analyst: adds 'Production Quality' and 'Speaker Expertise' instead of 'Source Quality' and 'Methodology'. The band display rule (Healthy (72) not raw 72) is portable. Output to .research/analysis/<media-slug>/ path is project-agnostic.

name: otb-challenger

notes: Has 'skills: [sonash-context]' frontmatter field. disallowedTools: Agent. model: sonnet. maxTurns: 25. Tools: Read, Write, Grep, Glob, WebSearch. Body is entirely project-agnostic. The 5 categories of blind spots (adjacent domains, inverted approaches, simpler alternatives, newer alternatives, hybrid approaches) are universal. Feasibility assessment (Relevance/Feasibility/Effort/Risk) is portable. Output to .research/<topic>/challenges/otb-N.md is project-agnostic. Max 8 alternatives anti-pattern is portable. The example block references 'cross-session state persistence' (generic) with git as an alternative — not SoNash-specific. Matches Piece 1a D2 record description. This agent is the sibling to contrarian-challenger — both are Phase 3 deep-research agents. contrarian-challenger critiques what was found; otb-challenger expands what was searched.

name: technical-writer
purpose: Documentation specialist for user guides, tutorials, README files, architecture docs, and content improvement. Understands SoNash doc standards including doc headers, version tables, prettier-ignore blocks, and docs:index generation.
sanitize_fields: description (SoNash doc standards reference)

notes: Has 'skills: [sonash-context]' frontmatter field. disallowedTools: Agent. model: inherit. maxTurns: 25. Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Grep, Glob. Consolidation target from markdown-syntax-formatter and prompt-engineer (both now deprecated stubs). The 5 Writing Principles (dual audience, lead with action, file:line citations, keep current, minimize token cost) are portable and valuable for JASON-OS. The version table convention is portable. The Document Types taxonomy (reference docs / operational docs / skill docs) maps well to JASON-OS. The SoNash-specific items: doc-header-config.json enforcement, DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md auto-generation, prettier-ignore blocks around tables. The anti-patterns (no docs unless requested, no aspirational content, no emojis) are directly applicable to JASON-OS.

name: test-engineer
purpose: Test automation and quality assurance specialist for test strategy, automation, coverage analysis, CI/CD testing, and quality engineering. Uses opus model. Covers Test Pyramid, multiple test types, and multi-framework knowledge.
sanitize_fields: description (SoNash testing context reference)

body (SoNash Overrides section: 5 SoNash-specific overrides — sanitizeError, no SQL interpolation, httpsCallable mocking, node:assert assertions, node:test imports)
body (example block: references SoNash-specific saveDailyLog Cloud Function and schemas.ts)
notes: Has 'skills: [sonash-context]' frontmatter field. model: opus (third opus agent alongside database-architect and security-auditor). No disallowedTools or maxTurns or color specified. Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash. This is the most template-heavy agent in the entire roster — body contains ~900 lines of code including a full TestSuiteManager class (jest-based), TestPatterns class, playwright.config.js, jest.config.js, PerformanceTestFramework, and a full CI/CD test workflow YAML. Notably, the main TestSuiteManager uses Jest (jest.fn(), npx jest) while the SoNash Overrides section explicitly overrides to node:test — this creates an internal contradiction. The body appears to be a scaffolded template agent (possibly imported from a generic template) with SoNash overrides appended. The SoNash Overrides section is explicit that it supersedes the generic examples below it. The generic test framework content (TestSuiteManager, TestPatterns, playwright config, jest config, PerformanceTestFramework) is highly portable as reference material. Key sanitization: remove SoNash Overrides section and replace with JASON-OS testing conventions.

name: audit-review-team
purpose: Coordinates audit execution workflows with a reviewer-fixer pipeline. Used for /audit-comprehensive and multi-target audit contexts (3+ artifacts). Decoupled from /skill-audit in v2.0.
notes: Team file uses prose-style markdown metadata (prettier-ignore block) NOT HTML comment block. Document Version: 2.0. Source: agent-env Phase 4, Step 4.2. HTML-comment parser does NOT apply — SCHEMA_SPEC Section 4 HTML-comment pattern does not match this file format. Parsed from markdown headers and table instead. Two members: reviewer (sonnet, Read/Grep/Glob/Bash) + fixer (sonnet, Read/Write/Edit/Bash/Grep/Glob). Persistence: ephemeral per-audit-invocation. Token cost: ~3x solo. Spawn trigger: /audit-* on 3+ artifacts OR /audit-comprehensive. NOT for: single file review, quick spot-checks, security-specific audits. Includes: cross-target pattern accumulation mechanism, 5-6 task max per teammate, no nested teams constraint. Integration: .claude/state/agent-token-usage.jsonl for token monitoring. Body references Decision #15 (frequency threshold for persistent teams). Version note: v2.0 decoupled from /skill-audit per skill-audit-batch-mode plan D11. The reviewer-fixer pipeline model is a portable design pattern for JASON-OS.

name: research-plan-team
purpose: Coordinates deep-research to deep-plan pipeline. Three-agent team: researcher (investigates), planner (opus, translates findings into plan), verifier (challenges claims). Used when research is L/XL complexity and will drive multi-session implementation.
notes: Team file uses prose-style markdown metadata (prettier-ignore block) NOT HTML comment block. Document Version: 1.0. Source: agent-env Phase 4, Step 4.2. HTML-comment parser does NOT apply — same format as audit-review-team. THREE members: researcher (sonnet, Read/Grep/Glob/Bash/WebSearch/WebFetch) + planner (opus, Read/Write/Edit/Bash/Grep/Glob) + verifier (sonnet, Read/Grep/Glob/Bash). Persistence: ephemeral per-research-plan-cycle. Token cost: ~4x solo. Spawn trigger: /deep-research + /deep-plan on same topic, L/XL complexity, multi-session plan. NOT for: simple research, /deep-plan on understood topic, 1-2 sub-questions. Decision #18 justifies opus for planner (plan quality is highest-leverage output). The researcher-planner-verifier cognitive separation rationale is valuable methodology. The progressive handoff pattern (researcher sends findings per sub-question, not all at once) is the key coordination insight. Includes detailed comparison table: Subagent vs Team approach. The token cost awareness section (4x solo cost justification criteria) is a portable governance pattern. The Coordination Model ASCII diagram shows inter-agent message flow. Task example references 'SoNash project' in example invocation (12 context providers) — sanitize_fields would cover this if portability required, but the methodology is project-agnostic.

name: commands-README
purpose: Migration notice: all command files deleted as of 2026-01-31 (Session #120). Skills in .claude/skills/ are now the only source. Contains command-to-skill mapping table.
notes: Commands folder is fully deprecated. YAML frontmatter has 'description: Commands Folder - DEPRECATED (Files Removed)'. Status DEPRECATED since 2026-01-31, Session #120. Migration completed: 10 commands mapped to skills (audit-code, audit-documentation, audit-performance, audit-process, audit-refactoring, audit-security, checkpoint, pr-review, session-begin, session-end). Command paradigm entirely replaced by skills paradigm. README is a redirect notice and might be removed in a future cleanup. For JASON-OS: the commands/ pattern is already replaced by skills/ — this confirms the architecture decision. The 10-command mapping list is a useful cross-reference for ensuring JASON-OS skill coverage. Commands folder in JASON-OS: empty (likely same state).

name: gsd-codebase-mapper
purpose: GSD codebase mapper. Explores codebase for a focus area (tech/arch/quality/concerns) and writes analysis documents to .planning/codebase/. Spawned by /gsd:map-codebase.
notes: GSD (Get Stuff Done) agent family. Located in .claude/agents/global/ subdir — NOT project-scoped .claude/agents/. model: sonnet. color: cyan. No maxTurns, disallowedTools. Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, Write. No 'skills: [sonash-context]' frontmatter — this is notable: global/ agents do NOT have sonash-context loaded. Writes to .planning/codebase/ (4 focus areas → 7 documents: STACK.md, INTEGRATIONS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, STRUCTURE.md, CONVENTIONS.md, TESTING.md, CONCERNS.md). Body is project-agnostic exploration methodology. The 4 focus areas (tech/arch/quality/concerns) and their output document mapping is a portable pattern. Consumed by /gsd:plan-phase and /gsd:execute-phase. Scope assigned as 'user' because .claude/agents/global/ likely maps to Claude Code user-level agent directory — these are portable across projects. No 'skills:' field confirms global agents are not SoNash-context-loaded.

name: gsd-debugger
purpose: GSD debugger. Investigates bugs using scientific method, manages debug sessions, handles checkpoints. Spawned by /gsd:debug and diagnose-issues workflow.
notes: GSD agent family. model: sonnet. color: orange. No maxTurns or disallowedTools. Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch. No 'skills: [sonash-context]' — global scope. Supports both interactive debugging and parallel UAT diagnosis modes. Scientific method approach (hypothesis testing). Manages debug session state files. Largest file in global/ subdir at 37,960 bytes — significantly more detailed than most project-scoped agents.

name: gsd-executor
purpose: GSD plan executor. Executes PLAN.md files atomically with per-task commits, deviation handling, checkpoint protocols, and SUMMARY.md creation. Spawned by /gsd:execute-phase.
notes: GSD agent family. model: sonnet. color: yellow. No maxTurns or disallowedTools. Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob. No 'skills: [sonash-context]' — global scope. Reads .planning/STATE.md for project state before any operation. Creates SUMMARY.md after execution. Atomic commits per task. Handles deviations and checkpoint pauses. The executor reads existing STATE.md to understand current position, accumulated decisions, blockers. State management pattern (.planning/ directory conventions) is a core GSD methodology.

name: gsd-integration-checker
purpose: GSD integration checker. Verifies cross-phase integration and E2E flows. Checks that phases connect properly and user workflows complete end-to-end.
notes: GSD agent family. model: sonnet. color: blue. No maxTurns or disallowedTools. Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob (read-only). No 'skills: [sonash-context]' — global scope. Checks cross-phase wiring (exports, APIs, data flows) and E2E user flow completion. Complements gsd-verifier (which checks single-phase goal achievement) — integration-checker focuses on between-phase wiring.

name: gsd-phase-researcher
purpose: GSD phase researcher. Researches how to implement a specific phase well. Produces RESEARCH.md consumed by gsd-planner. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase and /gsd:research-phase.
notes: GSD agent family. model: sonnet. color: cyan. No maxTurns or disallowedTools. Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*. MCP wildcard tool spec is notable — uses mcp__context7__* (glob) rather than listing specific Context7 tools. No 'skills: [sonash-context]' — global scope. Answers 'What do I need to know to PLAN this phase well?' Produces single RESEARCH.md. Sibling to gsd-project-researcher (which answers 'What does this domain ecosystem look like?').

name: gsd-plan-checker
purpose: GSD plan checker. Verifies that plans WILL achieve the phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase.
notes: GSD agent family. model: sonnet. color: green. No maxTurns or disallowedTools. Tools: Read, Bash, Glob, Grep (read-only). No 'skills: [sonash-context]' — global scope. Pre-execution quality gate: verifies plans before gsd-executor runs them. Complements gsd-verifier (which verifies AFTER execution) — plan-checker verifies BEFORE execution. Together they form a before/after verification loop.

name: gsd-planner
purpose: GSD planner. Creates executable phase plans with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase. Plans are prompts, not documents.
notes: GSD agent family. model: sonnet. color: green. No maxTurns or disallowedTools. Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Glob, Grep, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*. MCP wildcard. No 'skills: [sonash-context]' — global scope. Key philosophy: 'PLAN.md IS the prompt, not a document that becomes a prompt.' Plans are prompts for Claude executors. Solo developer + Claude workflow context (no teams, no ceremonies). Handles standard planning, gap closure planning (--gaps flag), and plan revision modes. Produces PLAN.md files. Largest agent in global/ at 44,624 bytes.

name: gsd-project-researcher
purpose: GSD project researcher. Researches domain ecosystem before roadmap creation. Produces files in .planning/research/. Spawned by /gsd:new-project and /gsd:new-milestone.
notes: GSD agent family. model: sonnet. color: cyan. No maxTurns or disallowedTools. Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, WebFetch, mcp__context7__*. MCP wildcard. No 'skills: [sonash-context]' — global scope. Answers 'What does this domain ecosystem look like?' (contrast with gsd-phase-researcher: 'What do I need to know to PLAN this phase?'). Produces research files in .planning/research/ consumed by gsd-research-synthesizer (which then feeds roadmap creation). Spawned 4 in parallel by /gsd:new-project (per gsd-research-synthesizer description: 'reads outputs from 4 parallel researcher agents').

name: gsd-research-synthesizer
purpose: GSD research synthesizer. Synthesizes research outputs from 4 parallel gsd-project-researcher agents into SUMMARY.md. Spawned by /gsd:new-project after researcher agents complete.
notes: GSD agent family. model: sonnet. color: purple. No maxTurns or disallowedTools. Tools: Read, Write, Bash. No 'skills: [sonash-context]' — global scope. Minimal toolset — only reads and writes. Aggregates 4 parallel researcher outputs into single SUMMARY.md. Smallest GSD agent at 7,367 bytes. The 4-parallel-researcher pattern is a notable GSD architecture decision.

name: gsd-roadmapper
purpose: GSD roadmapper. Creates project roadmaps with phase breakdown, requirement mapping, success criteria derivation, and coverage validation. Spawned by /gsd:new-project.
notes: GSD agent family. model: sonnet. color: purple. No maxTurns or disallowedTools. Tools: Read, Write, Bash, Glob, Grep. No 'skills: [sonash-context]' — global scope. Creates roadmaps mapping requirements to phases with goal-backward success criteria. Consumes SUMMARY.md from gsd-research-synthesizer. Upstream in the GSD new-project pipeline: project-researcher(x4) → research-synthesizer → roadmapper → planner → executor.

name: gsd-verifier
purpose: GSD phase verifier. Verifies that a phase achieved its GOAL, not just completed its TASKS. Goal-backward verification against actual codebase state, not SUMMARY.md claims.
notes: GSD agent family. model: sonnet. color: green. No maxTurns or disallowedTools. Tools: Read, Bash, Grep, Glob (read-only). No 'skills: [sonash-context]' — global scope. Core mandate: 'Do NOT trust SUMMARY.md claims. Verify what ACTUALLY exists in the code.' Creates VERIFICATION.md report. Pairs with gsd-plan-checker (pre-execution) to form a before/after gate. The 'actual codebase vs claimed SUMMARY' verification discipline is a key GSD quality pattern.
